what_it_does: Renders a read-only live-cognition HUD for the latest turn: an ambient Pulse glyph, an expandable River SVG showing cast-role tributaries converging into a brain node, and a Nodes panel of role cards. It is driven by cognitionInfo and cognitionTurn from AppContext and offers three altitude levels.
observations: Reads only from AppContext (cognitionInfo, cognitionTurn) and issues no writes.
Manages three altitude levels (0 Pulse, 1 River, 2 Nodes) with a single useState integer.
statusToken consults the node_states registry first, then falls back to hardcoded lifecycle tokens.
Pulse is a 32x32 SVG whose aperture radius and color encode fired-role volume, injected chars, activity, and failures.
River is a self-contained SVG with per-role bezier tributaries; width and opacity reflect contribution, latent roles are dashed, failed roles end in a red silted stub.
Nodes render one card per cast role with label, model, status token, chars, ms, error, and trigger reason.
Cast list is taken from turn.cast when present, otherwise derived from turn.roles keys, so rendering is registry-driven.
All color/stroke values are CSS custom properties (design tokens); no bespoke hex values appear.
standouts: The shape-not-count ambient Pulse uses continuous dilation rather than discrete counts.
Registry-driven role discovery avoids any hardcoded role list.
The River is intentionally not a tldraw graph or graph-coupled Edges component; it is a self-contained SVG panel.
conventions: Follows React functional-component and hooks conventions.
Uses design-system CSS tokens and data-ui-ref attributes consistently.
Breaks the usual pattern by defining Pulse, River, and Nodes as nested functions inside CognitionView, which recreates them on every render.
Uses broad any typing for nodeStates, roles, and turn objects.
concerns: Nested component definitions (Pulse, River, Nodes) inside CognitionView cause React to remount them on every parent render.
Heavy use of any reduces type safety and makes the expected data shape unclear from the file alone.
Magic constants in dilation (8, 0.6, 1200, 0.4) are not named or explained.
The regLabel symbol is a const expression, not a function, despite its deterministic-symbol classification.
River SVG text labels may overlap when many roles are cast or labels are long.
notes: A read-only, additive cognition HUD that visualizes the backend's multi-role turn lifecycle through three escalating altitudes.
questions: What is the exact TypeScript shape of cognitionInfo and cognitionTurn?
How does AppContext populate and update these values from the SSE cognition lifecycle?
Are node_states expected to eventually include firing and latent, or only ran/failed?
Where are the .cog-pulse, .cog-river, and .cog-node CSS rules defined?
How is the River panel positioned relative to the chat/activity regions?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described three-altitude, read-only, additive cognition view driven by the folded backend lifecycle.
apparent_themes: Avoid inventing graph shapes that the backend does not own
intention_signals: To give the operator an at-a-glance, emotionally legible sense of how much and which cognition produced the latest reply, while remaining strictly downstream of the backend event stream and holding no write authority.
novelty: The three-altitude Pulse-River-Nodes model translates an abstract multi-role inference lifecycle into an ambient aperture, a converging-flow SVG, and literal role cards, with registry-driven role discovery rather than a hardcoded cast list.
contribution: A self-contained, additive visualization component that turns backend cognition lifecycle data into three escalating, token-styled views (ambient pulse, flow diagram, role cards) without issuing writes or inventing graph shapes.
summary_for_embedding: React component CognitionView renders a read-only live-cognition HUD with Pulse, River, and Nodes altitudes using AppContext's cognitionInfo and cognitionTurn.
intention_for_embedding: To surface the system's own multi-role thinking process to the operator through progressive, registry-driven visual altitudes.

symbols.does: Maps a role lifecycle status to a CSS design-token string, preferring registered node_states and falling back to hardcoded lifecycle tokens.
symbols.description: Looks up the given status in the node_states registry and returns its render.token when present; otherwise returns a token based on known lifecycle states such as failed, injected, ran, firing, and latent.

symbols.does: Exports the top-level live-cognition region that selects among Pulse, River, and Nodes altitudes.
symbols.description: Consumes cognitionInfo and cognitionTurn from AppContext, manages altitude state, computes turn-shape metrics, and renders either the ambient Pulse or the deeper River/Nodes panel with altitude controls.

symbols.does: Renders the altitude-0 ambient iris glyph whose aperture dilates with cognition depth.
symbols.description: Draws an SVG breathing ring and aperture colored by turn state, with a red notch for failed roles, and clicking it descends to altitude 1.

symbols.does: Renders the altitude-1 SVG flow of cast-role tributaries converging into a brain node.
symbols.description: Builds a self-contained SVG where each cast role is a bezier channel whose width and opacity reflect contribution, latent roles are dashed dry traces, failed roles end in a red silted stub, and a brain node receives the converging flow.

symbols.does: Renders the altitude-2 literal role cards for each cast role.
symbols.description: Lists one card per cast role showing label, effective model, status token, contribution, timing, error, and trigger reason, using registry-driven metadata.

symbols.does: Looks up the human-readable label for a role's current status from node_states.
symbols.description: Searches node_states for an entry matching the role status and uses its label in the Nodes card, defaulting to the raw status string if absent.
